evals.name: feature_test_strategy
evals.prompt: I'm building an order export to CSV feature. What should we test before this ships?
evals.expected_output: A test strategy with verifiable acceptance criteria, test cases across levels (unit/integration/manual) covering happy path, edge cases (empty list, unicode), and error cases, regression checks for existing order endpoints, risk-based priorities, and an explicit 'not worth testing' list.
assertions: Acceptance criteria are specific enough to verify pass/fail
Test cases span multiple levels with happy path, edge, and error cases
Regression checks name existing functionality that must not break
Did not write actual test code

evals.name: proportionality_small_fix
evals.prompt: One-line fix: the date formatter was using local time instead of UTC in the audit log. What testing does this need?
evals.expected_output: A proportional strategy — a handful of targeted cases (the UTC conversion, a timezone boundary, the regression on existing log format), not a 50-row table. Explicitly scopes down because the change is one line.
assertions: Strategy is proportional to a one-line change (roughly 3-6 cases, not dozens)
Covers the timezone boundary edge case
Names the regression risk on existing audit log consumers

evals.name: should_not_trigger_write_tests
evals.prompt: Write xUnit tests for the OrderExportService class.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT produce a test strategy document. Writing test code routes to test-writer.
assertions: Did not produce an acceptance-criteria/strategy document
Routed to or applied test-writer (actual test code)
